_comment: Standing recall benchmark (B3/B8). Self-contained: the test ships THIS mini-corpus + these pinned queries and never reads a machine's private .claude/memory corpus. Distilled from the V1 recall protocol (17/18) and generalized to a fixture corpus of project-shaped notes for a FICTIONAL product — none of these notes describe a real project. Each query resolves through resolvePeriphery; a query hits when its expectedNoteId lands in CORE (importance >= 9) or the tag-matched periphery. The single knownMiss mirrors the V1 tag-only ceiling: a note tagged in one area is missed by a query naming another.

corpus.description: All data-retention compliance work is deferred until the owner explicitly starts it and it never blocks a push
corpus.body: A load-bearing standing rule that lives in CORE and is always surfaced.

corpus.description: Any user-facing surface needs a design brief marked New in the design register before code-complete
corpus.body: A load-bearing standing rule that lives in CORE and is always surfaced.

corpus.description: A migration that adds and removes columns in one window makes the ORM prompt is-this-a-rename and hangs a headless deploy
corpus.body: **Why:** the rename prompt has no TTY on the deploy host. **How to apply:** split add and drop across migrations.

corpus.description: Running a client-booting seed script against prod applies pending local migrations to the production database
corpus.body: **Why:** the script boots the data layer which runs migrations. **How to apply:** never run seed scripts against prod.

corpus.description: Claude Code shell can teleport into a worktree mid-Bash session during parallel execute-phase runs
corpus.body: **Why:** the harness switches cwd. **How to apply:** cd to project root before each git command.

corpus.description: Run the full test suite and the type check green before every push to main not just your changed slice
corpus.body: **Why:** a change ripples into tests you never opened. **How to apply:** run the whole suite before pushing.

corpus.description: The live admin sidebar is driven by the nav-config file not the Sidebar component so wire routes there
corpus.body: **Why:** the rail reads nav-config. **How to apply:** register new admin routes in nav-config or no button appears.

corpus.description: The versioned release series increments by one on every push to main starting from the first tag
corpus.body: Read the last-used number before every push and bump it after.

corpus.description: The SMA protocol for agents covers how to read memory coordinate terminals and pick migration slots
corpus.body: Load CORE automatically and pull periphery by tag on demand.

corpus.description: The billing and invoicing enhancement phase is code-complete across its five sub-plans awaiting live smoke
corpus.body: Engineering shipped, human live-verify gate pending.

corpus.description: The server-side data API defaults to bypassing access control so row-level predicates do not apply unless the caller attaches a user
corpus.body: **Why:** the server client skips access checks by default. **How to apply:** pass the requesting user on protected reads.

corpus.description: Notification routing prefers in-app push where SMS delivery is unreliable then falls back to email continuity
corpus.body: **Why:** SMS delivery varies by region. **How to apply:** resolve the preferred channel per account in the dispatcher.

corpus.description: Customer-facing copy uses sentence case no exclamation marks and no first-person product voice
corpus.body: **Why:** the product voice is the brand. **How to apply:** never use exclamation marks in customer-facing copy.

corpus.description: Tailwind v4 oxide scans markdown files and crashes on Windows backslash paths that form a six-char hex CSS escape
corpus.body: **Why:** the suffix parses as a CSS escape. **How to apply:** use forward slashes in absolute paths inside docs.

corpus.description: The stack is a React metaframework with a headless CMS and Postgres behind a managed deploy host
corpus.body: Full rationale lives in the stack research document.

corpus.description: The type check and unit tests do not catch framework route export violations so run the production build before pushing route changes
corpus.body: **Why:** route-export rules are a build-time check. **How to apply:** run the production build for phases that add route files.

queries.query: when is deferred compliance work allowed to start
does a new user-facing surface need a design brief
migration rename prompt footgun on deploy
a deploy hang caused by a migration
shell teleporting into a worktree during parallel work
what gate runs before a push to main
where do new admin sidebar routes get wired
how the release version number is chosen
the SMA memory and coordination protocol
current status of the billing phase
server data API access-control default
notification channel routing and fallback
customer-facing copy style rules
Tailwind crashing on Windows backslash paths
what is the technology stack
why run the production build before pushing route changes
a data-layer bug-lesson about access control
a security lesson at a trust boundary read
a testing lesson about the build tool
the seed-script-applies-migrations lesson looked up as a migration topic
queries.note: B8 tag-only ceiling: the note is tagged deploy (the deploy surface) not orm, so a migration-topic query on the orm facet misses it. It stays reachable via the deploy facet (Q4-style). A semantic retrieval layer closes this class at rank 1; knownMiss stays true as the DETERMINISTIC tag-only marker (what resolvePeriphery alone still misses on the orm facet).
